description:
Trigger Unity script recompilation and wait for it to complete.

Use this tool AFTER modifying C# script files (.cs) in the Unity project. Unity's auto domain reload is disabled by default — you MUST call this tool to apply your code changes.

What happens:
1. Unity Editor is brought to the foreground
2. Script compilation is triggered
3. If compilation succeeds: domain reload occurs (Unity plugin reconnects automatically)
4. If compilation fails: compile errors are returned

IMPORTANT:
- Always call this tool after editing .cs files before using unity_execute, so that your changes take effect.
- This tool may take 10-60 seconds depending on the project size.
- Do NOT call this tool while `editor_status` is `playing` or `playing_paused`.

project_path.description: Absolute path to the Unity project root. Required.

editor_status.description: The canonical Unity Editor status from the Environment section's `Unity Editor Status`. Allowed values: `disconnected`, `editing`, `playing`, `playing_paused`. You MUST read the current status and pass it exactly. `unity_recompile` only runs when the actual status is `editing`.
